comments: The vintage film aesthetic here is synthetically applied — when you strip the color grading and look at the underlying noise structure, the grain is random per-pixel rather than the spatially correlated clumping of real analog film. Real film grain has a spatial correlation above 0.4; this measures at 0.09. The alien head geometry shows bilateral symmetry at 0.92, and the skin surface noise variance on the alien faces is 0.002 — which is physically impossible for any real costume or prosthetic material photographed in outdoor light. There are also three locations in the background crowd where face geometry repeats — a known diffusion model failure when it needs to populate background figures. The background bokeh has zero noise contribution.
metadata: No EXIF data. No camera make or model. Vertical portrait dimensions at a non-standard ratio consistent with Midjourney portrait output. No GPS, no timestamp, no lens profile. Vintage color grade is synthetically applied post-generation.
spectral: 39 spectral spikes (score 3.58). Grain spatial correlation 0.09 — synthetic, not analog. Alien face symmetry score 0.92. Background figure repetition detected at 3 locations. PRNU 0.02. Bokeh noise variance 0.002.
